ego: noun. The self, especially with a sense of self-importance.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erg: noun. A unit of work or energy, being the amount of work done by a force of one dyne applied through a distance of one centimeter.
genro: noun. A body of elder statesmen of Japan, formerly used as informal advisors to the Emperor.
goer: noun. One who, or that which, goes.
goner: noun. Someone (or something) doomed; a hopeless case, especially someone who is bound to die soon.
goo: noun. Any semi- solid or liquid substance; especially one that is sticky, gummy or slippery, unpleasant, and of vague or unknown
goon: noun. A thug; a usually muscular henchman with little intelligence.
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gore: noun. Blood, especially that from a wound when thickened due to exposure to the air.
govern: verb. To make and administer the public policy and affairs of; to exercise sovereign authority in.
governor: noun. The chief executive officer of a first - level administrative division of a country.
groove: noun. A long, narrow channel or depression; e.g., such a slot cut into a hard material to provide a location for an engineering component,
groover: noun. One who grooves, or enjoys rhythmic music.
nog: noun. A wooden block, the size of a brick, built into a wall, as a hold for the nails of woodwork.
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
ogre: noun. A large, grotesque, brutish humanoid monster, typically marked by great strength and a propensity to kill or devour humans.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
orgone: noun. In the psychoanalytic theory of Wilhelm Reich, a form of sexual energy or life force distributed throughout the universe and
oven: noun. A chamber used for baking or heating.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.

voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
ergon: noun. Work, measured in terms of the quantity of heat to which it is equivalent.
geo: noun. An inlet, gully or cleft in the face of a cliff.
gorer: noun. Someone or something that gores (typically an animal that gores other creatures).
gove: noun. A mow; a rick for hay.
grr: interjection. Sound of an animal growl.
negro: adjective. Relating to a black ethnicity.
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
oreo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Oreo.
orogen: noun. Rock deformed by orogeny; a belt of such rock(s).
overgo: verb. To cross, go over (a barrier etc.); to surmount.
rego: noun. Registration for a motor vehicle.
roon: noun. A shred or remnant (usually of cloth).
vog: noun. Air pollution caused by substances (such as sulphur dioxide) emitted by a volcano.
